metadata.title: International Organizations, Defence, Books & Authors, World History

questions.question: Who is the author of the biography of Saint Chaitanya?
questions.explanation: Chaitanya Mahaprabhu, a renowned devotee of Lord Krishna and founder of Gaudiya Vaishnavism, had his life and teachings documented in a biography written by Krshnadas Kaviraj. This biography, entitled Chaitanya Charitamrita, provides detailed insights into his life, contributions, and teachings, validating Krshnadas Kaviraj as the author.

questions.question: Who among the following was the author of the book “Unhappy India” written as a response to the book “Mother India” ?
questions.explanation: “Unhappy India” was written by Lala Lajpat Rai. It was published in the year 1928. The book was a reply to “Mother India” written by Katherine Mayo.

questions.question: Which of the following organisation issue notices in the form of Green, Blue, Orange, Blue, Red and purple?
questions.explanation: An INTERPOL notice or International Notice is issued by INTERPOL to share critical crime-related information between its member countries. There are eight types, seven of which are known by their colour codes. Based on requests from National Central Bureaus (NCBs), the General Secretariat produces notices in all of the organization’s official languages: Arabic, English, French and Spanish. Besides these notices are used by the International Criminal Tribunals and the International Criminal Court to seek persons wanted for genocide, war crimes, and crimes against humanity.

questions.question: Who has been appointed as the new director of the United Nations World Tourism Organization?
questions.explanation: Kerala cadre senior IAS officer Suman Billa will join the United Nations World Tourism Organisation (UNWTO) at its headquarters in Madrid, Spain. A 1996 batch IAS officer, he will assume charge as Director, Technical Cooperation and Silk Road Development at D1 level at UNWTO, a specialised agency responsible for the promotion of responsible, sustainable and universally accessible tourism.

questions.question: Who is the author of “Cow in Our Economy”?
questions.explanation: Cow in Our Economy is written by Gandhian J C Kumarappa. He was an Indian economist and a close associate of Mahatma Gandhi.

questions.question: From which nation Benin got its independence in 1960?
questions.explanation: From the mid-17th century to the mid-19th century Portuguese Empire had a colonial presence in Benin. The Portuguese empire was engaged in the massive slave trade with Benin. This continued till the abolition of slavery through the Slave Trade Act, 1807 which weakened Benin’s (previously Dahomey) status. This lead to the emergence of French power in Benin’s soil during the mid-19th century. And finally, due to the anti-colonial stand of Beninese during the later years, France was forced to independence to Benin in 1960.

questions.question: Which day is celebrated as International Day for the Conservation of the Mangrove Ecosystem?
questions.explanation: 26 July is celebrated as the International Day for the Conservation of the Mangrove Ecosystem.

questions.question: Which of the following were sent to preach Christianity to the world?
questions.explanation: In Christian theology, Apostles, particularly the Twelve Apostles which are also known as the Twelve Disciples were the primary disciples of Jesus according to the New Testament. After the death of Jesus, these Twelve Apostles helped to spread Christianity.

questions.question: Which of the following Apostles did not know Jesus during his ministry and before his Crucifixion?
questions.explanation: Paul also called Saint pauls is generally considered one of the most important figures of the Apostolic Age. He did not know Jesus during his ministry and before his Crucifixion.

questions.question: Which of the following completed the first circumnavigation of the Earth?
questions.explanation: Juan Sebastián Elcano was a Spanish explorer. After Magellan’s death in the Philippines, Elcano took command of the carrack Victoria from the Moluccas to Sanlúcar de Barrameda in Spain. He completed the first circumnavigation of the Earth.
